---
title: "CO2 Emissions: Table, Trends, and Global Map"
author: "Mongal S Gurung"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Introduction

This report explores global CO2 emissions data from Gapminder. It presents an interactive table showing changes in emissions between 2000 and 2022, a line chart comparing emission trends in five selected countries from 1990 to 2022, and an animated choropleth map showing changes in CO2 emissions worldwide.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Load the data
emissions = pd.read_csv("data/co2_cons.csv")
```

# Data Preparation

```{python}
# Create table dataset
table_df = emissions[["name", "2000", "2022"]].copy()

# Calculate absolute change
table_df["Absolute Change"] = (
    table_df["2022"] - table_df["2000"]
)

# Calculate relative change
table_df["Relative Change"] = (
    table_df["Absolute Change"] / table_df["2000"]
) * 100

# Round relative change and add percentage sign
table_df["Relative Change"] = (
    table_df["Relative Change"]
    .round(0)
    .astype(str)
    + "%"
)

# Reshape emissions data from wide to long format
emissions_long = emissions.melt(
    id_vars=["geo", "name"],
    var_name="year",
    value_name="emissions"
)

# Convert year to numeric
emissions_long["year"] = pd.to_numeric(
    emissions_long["year"],
    errors="coerce"
)

# Convert emissions to numeric
emissions_long["emissions"] = pd.to_numeric(
    emissions_long["emissions"]
    .astype(str)
    .str.replace("−", "-")
)

# Keep years from 1990 to 2022
emissions_long_1990_2022 = emissions_long.query(
    "year >= 1990 and year <= 2022"
).copy()

# Select five countries using ISO3 country codes
emissions_long_subset = emissions_long_1990_2022.query(
    "geo in ['btn', 'ind', 'chn', 'usa', 'gbr']"
).copy()

# Create line chart
fig_chart = px.line(
    emissions_long_subset,
    x="year",
    y="emissions",
    color="name",
    title="CO2 Emissions Trends, 1990–2022",
    labels={
        "year": "Year",
        "emissions": "CO2 Emissions",
        "name": "Country"
    }
)

# Prepare ISO3 codes for mapping
emissions_long_1990_2022["country_code"] = (
    emissions_long_1990_2022["geo"].str.upper()
)

# Create animated choropleth map
fig_map = px.choropleth(
    emissions_long_1990_2022,
    locations="country_code",
    color="emissions",
    hover_name="name",
    animation_frame="year",
    title="Global CO2 Emissions, 1990–2022"
)
```

# CO2 Emissions Dashboard

The tabs below provide three complementary views of the CO2 emissions data. The table summarizes changes between 2000 and 2022, the chart compares trends across five selected countries, and the map shows the global distribution of emissions over time.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Table

The table presents CO2 emissions in 2000 and 2022 and the corresponding absolute and relative changes for each country.

```{python}
# Use Quarto's native engine to print dataframes natively
table_df
```

## Chart

The chart compares CO2 emission trends from 1990 to 2022 for Bhutan, India, China, the United States, and the United Kingdom.

```{python}
# Do not use .show() – just leave the object name to render cleanly into HTML
fig_chart
```

## Map

The animated map shows how the global distribution of CO2 emissions changed between 1990 and 2022. Use the animation controls to explore changes over time.

```{python}
# Do not use .show() – just leave the object name to render cleanly into HTML
fig_map
```

:::
